headline: CORN CRASHES TWO PERCENT ON FUND LIQUIDATION
subheadline: Planting progress hits 47%, exceeding weekly target as grains shed technical premium.
lead: Corn cratered $4.63, down 8¾ cents in the biggest single-session drop in three weeks, as funds liquidated 18,000 contracts and planting progress hit 47% to exceed the week's 45% target. The Belt is ahead of schedule, the weather premium is gone, and December corn at $4.87 is pricing normal yields with no buffer. Monday's question about hitting 45% by Friday just got answered with authority.
the_takeaway: Planting pace killed the weather premium; corn's pricing normal yields now.
teaser: Corn crashes as planting beats target, funds bail on weather premium that no longer exists.

one_number.context: USDA's Friday crop progress report showed corn planting at 47%, clearing this week's 45% threshold that would keep the Belt on the five-year average pace. The number killed the weather premium funds had been carrying for three weeks. When you're ahead of normal, normal weather doesn't add premium.

yesterdays_call.summary: Hogs' seasonal demand premium would build faster than supply could respond.
yesterdays_call.note: Hogs fell 1.1% instead, giving back yesterday's surge as protein demand cooled across the board.

sections.title: Corn Crashes on Planting Progress
sections.body: Corn crashed to $4.62¾, down 8¾ cents, as **USDA's crop progress report showed 47% planted**, exceeding the 45% target that would keep the Belt on schedule. Funds liquidated 18,000 contracts in the heaviest selling since April. December corn at $4.87 is now pricing normal weather and average yields with no cushion. **The weather premium that built over three weeks got erased in one session.** Technical selling accelerated through $4.70 support, and the next real floor is $4.55.
sections.bottom_line: Weather premium gone, corn pricing normal yields with no buffer.

sections.title: Soybeans Follow Corn Lower
sections.body: Soybeans fell to $11.84, down 12¼ cents, dragged by corn's technical breakdown and **lack of concrete details from Trump-Xi agricultural discussions**. November beans at $11.74 held the 200-day moving average but barely. Fund positioning remains net long 64,000 contracts, but today's selling pressure suggests that's unwinding. **China will continue buying primarily from Brazil** until new trade commitments emerge with actual tonnage numbers.
sections.catalyst: No concrete ag purchase details from Trump-Xi Beijing meeting
sections.bottom_line: Following corn's lead lower, waiting for China trade specifics.

sections.title: Livestock Gives Back Gains
sections.body: Live cattle held $246.40, up a dime, but feeders dropped to $358.23 on technical selling after Thursday's rally faded. **Hogs crashed to $104.78**, down 1.1%, giving back yesterday's three-percent surge as protein demand cooled. JBS reported $221 million Q1 profit but noted extremely challenging U.S. beef environment with North American margins down 3.7%. **The hog-cattle rotation that drove yesterday's action reversed completely.**
sections.catalyst: JBS Q1 results show challenging U.S. beef margins down 3.7%
sections.bottom_line: Protein rally fades, yesterday's rotation completely reversed.
sections.vs_yesterday: Livestock: hog strength from yesterday completely unwound.

outside_the_pit.title: H9N2 Bird Flu Strain Shows Greater Infection Potential
outside_the_pit.body: New research shows H9N2 avian influenza demonstrates significantly greater ability to replicate, spread and infect chickens compared to current strains, with more spillover potential to other species. **The timing couldn't be worse** with protein markets already under pressure from margin compression.

outside_the_pit.title: Middle East Conflict Squeezes Global Fertilizer Production
outside_the_pit.body: Fertilizer Institute CEO tells Senate Ag Committee that disruptions from U.S.-Iran conflict continue affecting global markets, with tighter supplies resulting from production outages. **Spring application season is feeling the squeeze** just as planting accelerates above normal pace.

outside_the_pit.title: House Passes Year-Round E15 Bill Despite Senate Uncertainty
outside_the_pit.body: Farm groups praised House passage of legislation allowing year-round nationwide E15 sales, but challenges await in the Senate. **The ethanol grind is already pricing the expectation** even as political hurdles remain substantial.

spread_to_watch.label: December corn / July corn carry
spread_to_watch.commentary: The carry blew out to 24 cents as old crop crashed harder than new crop, **exactly what happens when weather premium evaporates**. Widening carry says the market believes harvest will be normal to large, no shortage premium needed.

basis.headline: Eastern Belt basis weakening as planting surge reduces urgency
basis.body: **Eastern Belt corn basis gave back 3-5 cents** as the planting progress report removed weather anxiety that had been supporting local premiums. Western Belt stayed soft as expected. **Producers with old-crop storage watching that window close** as the futures discount deepens and basis follows futures lower.

weekly_thread.question: Will corn planting hit 45% by Friday to stay on the five-year average?
weekly_thread.status_text: Answered with authority: 47% planted exceeds the 45% target. The Belt is ahead of schedule, the weather premium is dead, and corn's pricing normal yields with no cushion.

the_more_you_know.title: How planting progress kills weather premiums overnight
the_more_you_know.body: Today's 47% corn planted number demonstrates how **a single USDA report can evaporate weeks of weather premium**. When planting runs ahead of the five-year average, the market stops pricing weather risk and starts pricing normal yields. **The 8¾ cent corn crash wasn't about weather turning bad, it was about weather risk turning irrelevant.** Once you're ahead of normal pace, normal weather becomes bearish, not neutral. The premium funds carried for three weeks got destroyed in three hours of selling.

watch_list.desc: Weekly export sales: corn under 800K MT confirms domestic focus
Crop progress update: soybean planting above 35% removes more weather risk
EIA petroleum status: crude builds above 2M barrels pressure energy complex
WASDE: corn yield estimate above 180 bushels/acre locks in bearish tone

daily_quote.text: In the midst of chaos, there is also opportunity.

source_summary: USDA Crop Progress, CME futures settlements, fund positioning data, JBS earnings, Trump-Xi summit readout
